pull_policy.expected_next_data_note: Expect the next synced dataset on the next 30-minute pull. Expect material new trading evidence only after the runtime records the first closed trades or structural alpha samples.
manual_pull_triggers: Immediately after any deploy or service restart.
After any remote incident, kill event, or unexpected runtime stop.
Before writing any daily, investor, or operator summary.

velocity_forecast.definition: Operator forecast for annualized return run-rate on currently tracked capital; not realized performance.
velocity_forecast.basis: Best guess assumes one more focused engineering session clears the immediate deployment blockers and gets a small live or shadow sleeve collecting calibration data.
assumptions: jj-live can resume without new safety or import failures.
A-6 and B-1 killed 2026-03-13; engineering capacity reallocated to BTC5 optimization and Kalshi.
The first resumed sleeve starts producing decision-grade telemetry quickly enough to move us off a zero run-rate.
invalidators: If jj-live remains paused after the next focused session, the 10% target is too aggressive.
A-6 and B-1 killed 2026-03-13 with zero density; run-rate forecast now depends solely on BTC5 sleeve performance.
If resumed runtime still generates zero closed trades, annualized return remains effectively unobservable.

blockers: BTC5 promotion gate currently fails (hold at $5/trade).
Need sustained fill evidence and >7 days of data before any size increase.
exit_criteria: Keep BTC5 at $5/trade until reports/btc5_promotion_gate.json shows overall_gate=true.
Require >=7 days of evidence with stable fill quality before proposing any scale-up.
Continue wallet-vs-local reconciliation so runtime contracts stay truthful.
